other_observers: None.; Yes. I'm the only one who saw it.
specific_location: San Luis Lake, at the inlet canal on the northwest side of the lake
description: At closest observation (~125 feet through 32x scope), I was able to see the following: medium-size shorebird, somewhat pot-bellied, but generally sleek in proportions; dark above, with complex striations of brown-buff and gray-black; densely streaked breast, mainly brown, but black-brown at lowest edge, contrasting very sharply with belly; slightly decurved, medium-length bill, pale yellow-orange at base; straw-yellow legs; primaries extending to tail tip.Behaviors: Feeding fairly constantly at water's edge, and sometimes right in the water; probed in water up to at least half the bill length. Not especially skittish. The only things preventing closer approach were (a) an inlet canal and (b) a state parks official who was about to give me the heave-ho.Call: none heard.Plumage: alternate adult
